news.t: Asian Currencies Consolidate Before Decision by Warsh-Led FOMC
Malaysia says it is seeking new sources of fuel amid energy crunch
Malaysia steps on gas, cuts coal use as power demand surges to record
Malaysia Central Bank Holds Steady as It Gauges Impact of Mideast War

holdings.infoBox: EWM is an equity ETF with a total of 28 individual holdings. The top holdings are Public Bank Berhad stock at 15.15%, Malayan Banking Berhad at 13.27%, CIMB Group Holdings Berhad at 12.47%, Tenaga Nasional Berhad at 8.15%, and Press Metal Aluminium Holdings Berhad at 7.17%.

overview.description: The iShares MSCI Malaysia ETF (EWM) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the MSCI Malaysia index, a market-cap-weighted index of Malaysian stocks primarily traded on the Kuala Lumpur Stock Exchange. EWM was launched on Mar 12, 1996 and is issued by BlackRock.

data.title: Asian Currencies Consolidate Before Decision by Warsh-Led FOMC
data.text: Asian currencies consolidated against the dollar before first decision by a FOMC led by Chairman Kevin Warsh later.

data.title: Malaysia says it is seeking new sources of fuel amid energy crunch
data.text: Malaysia is seeking new sources of fuel amid a ​global crunch caused by the war in ‌Iran, though any supplies would need to be able to be processed by the country's ​refineries, the Economy Minister s...

data.title: Malaysia steps on gas, cuts coal use as power demand surges to record
data.text: Malaysia ​is tapping its offshore gas reserves to meet record power demand driven by a searing heatwave and ‌data centres, even as countries across Asia burn more coal to make up for liquefied natural...

data.title: Malaysia Central Bank Holds Steady as It Gauges Impact of Mideast War
data.text: The central bank noted uncertainties arising from the ongoing Middle East conflict, saying the economic impact will depend on how the situation develops.

data.title: My Top 10 Stock Picks for 2025
data.text: Each year, we ask an expert to pick 10 stocks that have the potential to beat the market over the next 12 months. Here are his choices for 2025.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia CEO on making Malaysian markets attractive
data.text: Bursa Malaysia CEO Muhamad Umar Swift talks about why Southeast Asian companies are increasingly turning to Malaysia to list or improve liquidity.
